lede: A look at March 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Claude Bourgelat and Quentin Tarantino, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Situ Gintung
events.desc: The dam forming Situ Gintung, an artificial lake in Indonesia, fails, killing at least 99 people.

events.title: Food and Drug Administration
events.desc: The Food and Drug Administration approves Viagra for use as a treatment for erectile dysfunction, the first pill to be approved for this condition in the United States.

events.title: Jiang Zemin
events.desc: Jiang Zemin is appointed President of China.

events.title: Solidarity (Polish trade union)
events.desc: The Solidarity movement in Poland stages a warning strike, in which at least 12 million Poles walk off their jobs for four hours.

events.title: Washington Metro
events.desc: The first section of the Washington Metro opens to the public.

events.title: Nikita Khrushchev
events.desc: Nikita Khrushchev becomes Chairman of the Council of Ministers of the Soviet Union.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Yugoslav Air Force officers topple the pro-Axis government in a bloodless coup.

events.title: Philippine–American War
events.desc: Philippine–American War: Emilio Aguinaldo, leader of the First Philippine Republic, is captured by the Americans.

events.title: 1638 Calabrian earthquakes
events.desc: The first of four destructive Calabrian earthquakes strikes southern Italy. Measuring magnitude 6.8 and assigned a Mercalli intensity of XI, it kills 10,000–30,000 people.

events.title: Charles I of England
events.desc: Charles I becomes King of England, Scotland and Ireland as well as claiming the title King of France.

births.name: Quentin Tarantino
births.line: American director, producer, screenwriter and actor

births.name: James Callaghan
births.line: English lieutenant and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: Mariano Rajoy
births.line: Spanish lawyer and politician, Prime Minister of Spain

deaths.text: Billy Wilder, Austrian-born American director, producer, and screenwriter
Yuri Gagarin, Russian colonel, pilot, and astronaut
Rupert of Salzburg, Austrian bishop and saint

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alexander, a Pannonian soldier, martyred in 3rd century.
Christian feast day: Charles Henry Brent (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Gelasius, Archbishop of Armagh
